questions.question: A learner says caste matters in India only as an old ritual identity. Which response gives the best sociological correction?
options: Caste can operate as identity, social network, and resource-access marker in different contexts.
Caste is relevant mainly in religious ceremonies and has little link with social opportunity.
Caste works in the same way across regions because its rules are fixed everywhere.
Caste has been removed from public life, so it can be studied only as history.

questions.question: Statement I: Indian society has unity as well as diversity. Statement II: Diversity means social groups cannot share common institutions. Which option is most accurate?
options: Statement I is valid, but Statement II mistakes diversity for complete separation.
Both statements are valid because shared institutions disappear in a diverse society.
Statement I is invalid because diversity prevents any form of social unity.
Statement II is valid because common institutions make diversity sociologically irrelevant.
